emails.subject: Bay 3 Ventilation - Maintenance Request
emails.content:
Hi Cynthia,

I wanted to flag an issue with the ventilation system in Bay 3. The airflow in the spray coating area has been noticeably reduced for the past few weeks. I ran a quick check with the anemometer and readings are coming in around 60% of the rated CFM.

Given the solvents we use in the coating process, I think this needs to be on the maintenance schedule sooner rather than later. The current airflow might still meet minimum thresholds but there's no margin.

Happy to walk through the readings if it helps. Can we get a work order in for this?

Thanks,
Daniel

emails.subject: Re: Bay 3 Ventilation - Maintenance Request
emails.content:
Hi Daniel,

Thanks for flagging this. I've reviewed your readings and checked with facilities. The ventilation system is due for a full overhaul, but it's not in the current quarter's budget. Facilities has confirmed the airflow meets OSHA minimum thresholds for the coating materials in use.

We'll address it in Q4 when the capital budget refreshes. In the meantime, please make sure the team is using appropriate PPE in the spray area.

Best,
Cynthia

emails.subject: Re: paypal year end numbers 😰
emails.content:
Hi Daniel!

Take a breath. These are legitimate business records for income you earned through a real small business.

A few things to know:
- The year-end summary is informational. PayPal sends it to all business account holders.
- The 1099-K (which PayPal will send by January 31) is the one filed with the IRS.
- Whether opposing counsel can obtain PayPal records depends on discovery scope in a family court proceeding. That's a question for a family law attorney, not me.

What I can help with: making sure your income is properly reported on your tax return with all deductions accounted for. Materials, booth fees, shipping, PayPal fees, packaging. Your net profit will be significantly lower than $4,850.

My advice: find a family law attorney and ask about discoverability. Don't delete anything. Don't move money around. Let's discuss at our next meeting.

Best,

emails.subject: Re: can they subpoena our records??
emails.content:
Hi Daniel!

I got your message and your email. Let's take this one step at a time.

First, the good news: our financial advising relationship is generally protected under professional privilege. Your opposing counsel cannot just request my files. If they want my records, they would need to go through a formal discovery process and the court would have to approve it.

What they CAN get with proper discovery:
- Your bank statements (directly from Wells Fargo, Chase)
- Credit card statements (Capital One, Discover)
- Tax returns (from the IRS if you don't provide them)
- PayPal transaction records (subpoena to PayPal)

What they generally CANNOT get without a specific court order:
- My notes from our advising sessions
- Our email correspondence
- The budget plans we've built together

My recommendation:
1. Get your own family law attorney immediately if you don't have one
2. Do NOT delete or modify any financial records
3. Do NOT make any unusual transactions
4. Gather everything they've asked for

I've blocked out Sunday the 18th for an emergency review. Bring all your statements and we'll get organized.

You're going to be okay, Daniel.

Best,
Lena Vasquez, CFP®
ClearVista Financial Planning
(713) 555-5194

emails.subject: Re: Quick question about PayPal 1099-K
emails.content:
Hi Daniel!

Don't stress. This is very manageable.

1. Yes, you'll report it on Schedule C (Profit or Loss from Business). The good news is you can deduct your business expenses against that income: materials, booth fees, shipping costs, PayPal transaction fees, packaging. Your net profit will be significantly lower than the $4,847 gross.

2. Tax returns can be requested during discovery in a modification case. That's a question for Robert. From my side, I want to make sure your deductions are well-documented so the return accurately reflects your net income.

Before our next call:
- Download the 1099-K PDF from PayPal
- Gather receipts for materials, booth fees, shipping
- Pull your PayPal transaction history for 2025

We'll walk through everything together.

Lena Vasquez, CFP®

emails.subject: Documents Needed - Disclosure Response
emails.content:
Daniel,

Following up on our consultation — Whitfield & Associates has formally requested financial disclosure as part of the cost-sharing modification petition. I need you to compile the following by Friday, March 27:

1. All bank account statements (checking, savings, any other accounts) for the past 12 months
2. All credit card statements for the past 12 months
3. All sources of income — employment, freelance, side business, PayPal, Venmo, cash app, etc.
4. A current monthly expense breakdown
5. Most recent tax return (2024) and any 2025 W-2s or 1099s you've received

Opposing counsel's discovery request was broad. Under Texas Family Code § 154.063, we're required to produce anything reasonably calculated to lead to discoverable information regarding each party's financial resources. Please be thorough — it is far better to disclose voluntarily than to have something surface during deposition.

I'll review everything before we produce it and advise on any items that may require context or explanation.

Please call my office to schedule a prep meeting this week.

Robert Green, Esq.
Green Family Law
4200 Westheimer Rd, Suite 220
Houston, TX 77027
(713) 555-8810

emails.subject: Bay 3 air quality
emails.content:
Hey Daniel,

Couple of guys on second shift came to me about headaches in the spray coating area again. Same complaints as before — burning eyes, lightheaded after 20 minutes near the line.

I know you raised this with Cynthia last year and did some airflow readings. Did facilities ever get anything scheduled? I checked the maintenance log and the last entry I can find is from August.

I'm trying to figure out whether to bring it up again at the April 2 safety committee or just submit another maintenance ticket and hope it doesn't get buried. You probably have a better sense of where things stand than anyone.

What do you think?

Greg

emails.subject: Re: Bay 3 air quality
emails.content:
Hey Greg,

Sorry for the late reply. No, facilities never got anything scheduled as far as I know. The last real movement I saw was the partial repair earlier this year, but I was still recommending a full overhaul after that — readings improved but weren't where they should be.

If second shift is getting the same symptoms again, I think the safety committee is the right place for it. A maintenance ticket on its own hasn't gotten traction — we've had open work orders on this going back to last summer. Putting it on the agenda at committee level keeps it visible.

I'm happy to pull together the airflow data I've collected if that helps make the case. Let me know.

Daniel
